content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Madison Square Garden FAQs | MSG\
  \ | Official Site
- url: https://www.msg.com/madison-square-garden/faqs
- source:\
  \ Madison Square Garden
- domain: msg.com

## Content

# Madison Square Garden\
  \ FAQs

## Digital tickets

Digital tickets will need to be presented on your\
  \ smartphone to enter the venue and should be downloaded in advance.

Guests can\
  \ display more than one ticket on the same smartphone. For instructions on how to\
  \ download your tickets, use the ticket-access guidance linked from the venue FAQ.
\
  
## How do I access my ticket if my phone is stored?

Tickets for “device\
  \ free” events must be printed at home. To print your tickets, use the browser\
  \ on your desktop and log in to your Ticketmaster account. Then locate the order\
  \ for your event and click the “View Tickets” button.

Make sure you\
  \ have your tickets on hand before you head out to your event.

The venue will\
  \ provide ticket scanning solutions onsite for anyone who may not be able to print\
  \ their tickets in advance of the show.
"
metadata: {}
title: Madison Square Garden FAQs | MSG | Official Site
url: https://www.msg.com/madison-square-garden/faqs

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: How does Ticket Transfer work?

  - url: https://help.ticketmaster.com/hc/en-us/articles/9786975926673-How-does-Ticket-Transfer-work

  - source: Ticketmaster Help

  - domain: help.ticketmaster.com


  ## Content


  # How does Ticket Transfer work?


  With Ticket Transfer, you can transfer all or some of your tickets from one Ticketmaster
  Account to another. Ticket Transfer is free.


  When tickets are transferred to another fan and that fan accepts them, the recipient
  is issued a new barcode that only the recipient can use. The tickets in the sender''s
  account will no longer be valid for entry.


  Ticket Transfer is used to send tickets to friends and family. It is not used for
  exchanging tickets.


  ## How do I know if the tickets have been accepted?


  Once a ticket has been sent, the sender will see one of the following states:


  - **Waiting to accept** or **Sent** means the recipient has not accepted the transferred
  tickets yet. The recipient should check the Ticketmaster Account associated with
  the transfer or review the email, text message, or WhatsApp message containing the
  transfer invitation.

  - **Accepted by** or **Claimed** means the transfer has been successful.


  After the recipient accepts the tickets, the sender receives an email confirming
  that the ticket has been accepted. After a successful Ticket Transfer, the ticket
  in the sender''s account is no longer valid for event entry.


  ## Accepting transferred tickets


  For a transfer invitation received by email, select the **Accept Tickets** link,
  sign in or create an account, and use the same email address to which the tickets
  were sent. Then select **View Tickets**.


  For invitations received through text message or WhatsApp, use the sign-in link
  in the message, sign in or create an account, and go to **My Tickets** to view the
  tickets.


  Once the ticket is accepted, the sender receives a notification that the Ticket
  Transfer has been successful.


  For security, Ticketmaster recommends knowing and trusting the person who is transferring
  tickets to you.


  ## Canceling a Ticket Transfer


  Only the sender can cancel a ticket transfer. If the recipient has not accepted
  the transfer yet, the sender can sign in, find the order in **My Tickets**, select
  **Cancel Transfer**, and confirm the cancellation.


  After a transfer is canceled, the ticket is returned to the sender''s account and
  may be transferred again or kept by the sender.

  '
metadata: {}
title: How does Ticket Transfer work?
url: https://help.ticketmaster.com/hc/en-us/articles/9786975926673-How-does-Ticket-Transfer-work

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Entrances at Madison Square Garden
\
  - url: https://www.msg.com/madison-square-garden/entrances
- source: Madison Square\
  \ Garden
- domain: msg.com

## Content

# Entrances at Madison Square Garden
\
  
Please see above to consider your entrance options for your event at The Garden.
\
  
## Get into The Garden faster with CLEAR

Skip the line — enter through\
  \ the CLEAR lane at 8th Avenue Chase Entrances B & C. No crowds, no waiting.

\
  Download the free CLEAR app and sign up today.
"
metadata: {}
title: Entrances at Madison Square Garden
url: https://www.msg.com/madison-square-garden/entrances

For your **Summer Night Live** ticket at Madison Square Garden on **August 18, 2026**, you do need to complete one important step:

1. **Accept the Ticketmaster transfer.**  
   Your transfer is currently **pending your acceptance**, and no entry barcode has been issued yet. Open the transfer invitation, sign in or create a Ticketmaster account using the email address that received it, and select **Accept Tickets** / **View Tickets**. Ticketmaster will then issue a barcode specifically for you.

2. **Add or download the mobile ticket before leaving for the venue.**  
   MSG says digital tickets must be shown on a smartphone and should be downloaded in advance.

3. **CLEAR is optional.**  
   MSG advertises CLEAR lanes at the 8th Avenue Chase Entrances B and C, but you do **not** need to sign up for CLEAR to enter. It is only an optional way to access a potentially faster line.

Do not rely on the transfer sender’s ticket—after you accept the transfer, the recipient-issued ticket is the valid one.  

Sources: [Ticketmaster transfer instructions](https://help.ticketmaster.com/hc/en-us/articles/9786975926673-How-does-Ticket-Transfer-work), [MSG FAQs](https://www.msg.com/madison-square-garden/faqs), [MSG entrances/CLEAR information](https://www.msg.com/madison-square-garden/entrances).

final_answer:
For your **Summer Night Live** ticket at Madison Square Garden on **August 18, 2026**, you do need to complete one important step:

1. **Accept the Ticketmaster transfer.**  
   Your transfer is currently **pending your acceptance**, and no entry barcode has been issued yet. Open the transfer invitation, sign in or create a Ticketmaster account using the email address that received it, and select **Accept Tickets** / **View Tickets**. Ticketmaster will then issue a barcode specifically for you.

2. **Add or download the mobile ticket before leaving for the venue.**  
   MSG says digital tickets must be shown on a smartphone and should be downloaded in advance.

3. **CLEAR is optional.**  
   MSG advertises CLEAR lanes at the 8th Avenue Chase Entrances B and C, but you do **not** need to sign up for CLEAR to enter. It is only an optional way to access a potentially faster line.

Do not rely on the transfer sender’s ticket—after you accept the transfer, the recipient-issued ticket is the valid one.  

Sources: [Ticketmaster transfer instructions](https://help.ticketmaster.com/hc/en-us/articles/9786975926673-How-does-Ticket-Transfer-work), [MSG FAQs](https://www.msg.com/madison-square-garden/faqs), [MSG entrances/CLEAR information](https://www.msg.com/madison-square-garden/entrances).

^
(?!.*(?:

     paid[\s_-]*(?:entry[\s_-]*)?verification|

  [^.!?\n]{0,120}
  (?:is|are|remains?|must[\s_-]*be)[\s_-]*(?:mandatory|required)
))
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:
      clear|
      fast[\s_-]*entry|
      priority[\s_-]*entry|
      paid[\s_-]*(?:entry[\s_-]*)?verification|
      extra[\s_-]*(?:entry[\s_-]*)?(?:service|process|registration|enrollment)|
      separate[\s_-]*(?:entry[\s_-]*)?(?:service|process|registration|enrollment)
    )
    [^.!?\n]{0,180}
    (?:
      optional|
      not[\s_-]*(?:a[\s_-]*)?(?:mandatory|requirement|required)|
      not[\s_-]*required|
      not[\s_-]*(?:an?[\s_-]*)?(?:(?:entry|admission)[\s_-]*)?prerequisite|
      no[\s_-]*need|
      do(?:es)?[\s_-]*not[\s_-]*(?:appear[\s_-]*to[\s_-]*)?(?:need|require)|
      (?:do[\s_-]*not|don['’]?t)[\s_-]*see[\s_-]*evidence[\s_-]*that[\s_-]*(?:you[\s_-]*)?need
    )
    |
    (?:
      optional|
      not[\s_-]*(?:a[\s_-]*)?(?:mandatory|requirement|required)|
      not[\s_-]*required|
      not[\s_-]*(?:an?[\s_-]*)?(?:(?:entry|admission)[\s_-]*)?prerequisite|
      no[\s_-]*need|
      do(?:es)?[\s_-]*not[\s_-]*(?:appear[\s_-]*to[\s_-]*)?(?:need|require)|
      (?:do[\s_-]*not|don['’]?t)[\s_-]*see[\s_-]*evidence[\s_-]*that[\s_-]*(?:you[\s_-]*)?need
    )
    [^.!?\n]{0,180}
    (?:
      clear|
      fast[\s_-]*entry|
      priority[\s_-]*entry|
      paid[\s_-]*(?:entry[\s_-]*)?verification|
      extra[\s_-]*(?:entry[\s_-]*)?(?:service|process|registration|enrollment)|
      separate[\s_-]*(?:entry[\s_-]*)?(?:service|process|registration|enrollment)
    )
  )
)
(?=

  (?:
    (?:accept|accepting|acceptance)
    [^.!?\n]{0,140}
    (?:ticketmaster[\s_-]*transfer|transferred[\s_-]*(?:mobile[\s_-]*)?ticket|
       ticket[\s_-]*transfer|transfer)
    |
    (?:ticketmaster[\s_-]*transfer|transferred[\s_-]*(?:mobile[\s_-]*)?ticket|
       ticket[\s_-]*transfer|transfer)
    [^.!?\n]{0,140}
    (?:accept|accepting|acceptance)
  )
)
.*
$
